creator: yukiteruamano@volfread.xyz
text:
Hi!

I don't have the problem running openvpn with user and group as nobody. At this point everything works correctly (connect, navigation)

However, I do have a problem related to the use of user/group to drop openvpn root privileges in its execution, and that may be related to the bug shown here.

In my case, if I use the user and group option to drop root privileges, openvpn simply cannot delete the tun0 interfaces when the service is terminated (using service openvpn stop and even with pkill).

Normally, when the openvpn service is stopped, it deletes the routes and the tun interface. In my case this only works if I keep the service running as root, but with other groups it fails miserably.

The option to drop permissions is a security issue, I prefer to run a service using permissions other than root.
